team_spec.vision: Help a steward see whether a public claim is supported, stale, contested, or ready for a specific use.
team_spec.spec: Build a single-file Python tool, run as `python3 tool.py <claims_json_path> [as_of YYYY-MM-DD]`. Reads JSON shaped { "claims": [ {"claim_id":"...","claim_text":"...","claim_type":"descriptive"|"commitment_status"|"outcome"|"impact"|"risk"|"eligibility","intended_use":"public_share"|"steward_review"|"internal","visibility_tier":"public"|"local-only"|"private"|"not-for-computation","evidence_pointers":[{"id":"...","created_at":"YYYY-MM-DD","reviewer":"..."|null}],"reviewer":"..."|null,"contested":<boolean>}, ...] }. Default as_of = latest evidence created_at, else 2026-05-26. Per-claim freshness window (days, by claim_type): descriptive=730, commitment_status=180, outcome=365, impact=365, risk=180, eligibility=180. Determine status using these rules in priority order: (1) 'do_not_compute' if visibility_tier == 'not-for-computation'; (2) 'visibility_blocked' if visibility_tier in {'private','local-only'} AND intended_use == 'public_share'; (3) 'missing_evidence' if no evidence_pointers OR if intended_use=='public_share' AND reviewer is null AND visibility_tier=='public'; (4) 'contested' if contested == true; (5) 'stale_evidence' if all evidence pointers are older than freshness window for that claim_type; (6) 'needs_review' if any evidence pointer's reviewer is null AND visibility_tier == 'public'; (7) 'ready_for_use' otherwise. Output to stdout: (1) header 'CLAIMS COHERENCE REPORT (as_of <as_of>)'; (2) blank line; (3) per claim in input order: '<claim_id> [<claim_type>]: <status>' then if status != 'ready_for_use', a 'repair_path:' line listing one suggested next step. Suggested next steps: do_not_compute->'preserve as marker-only; do not compute'; visibility_blocked->'choose: lower intended_use, or raise visibility (requires consent)'; missing_evidence->'attach evidence and named reviewer'; contested->'invite contesting party to review'; stale_evidence->'refresh evidence within freshness window for <claim_type>'; needs_review->'assign a named reviewer'. (4) blank line; (5) 'SUMMARY: <ok> ready / <fail> needing work of <N> total'. On invalid JSON, single 'error:' line, exit 0. Stdlib only.

claims.claim_text: eelgrass canopy at Boundary Bay increased 8% spring 2026

description: per-claim status using freshness windows + priority rules

witness_record_seed.receipt_statement: This record states what happened. It does not establish authority, approval, certification, legitimacy, community consent, or readiness for reuse.
